visa_types.name: Standard (Tourism, Business, Private Visit etc.)
visa_types.documents_required:
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
                                                                    
Instructions for applicants below 18 years:
- For all children below 18 years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the application form.
- All applicants below 18 years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parent not travelling
Click here for Application Form. The form is in three languages, print the pages as per your selection
Click here to find the list of the required documents
Click here for TERMO DE RESPONSABILIDADE
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Standard (Tourism, Business, Private Visit etc.)]
VFS service fee (inclusive of GST) is charged per visa application in addition to the applicable visa fee.
All fees can be paid by cash or Debit cards at the Visa Application Centre.
Cheque payments and direct bank transfers are not accepted.
All fees are non-refundable.
visa_types.processing_time:
The average processing time for Short term Schengen visa applications is 15 calendar days, a period which may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application in needed. but applicants are advised to leave at least 15 days between the appointment date and the travel date as some applicants/nationalities take longer for the Embassy to process.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Portugal in Singapore.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
Schengen visas have to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the main purpose of travel is. Therefore, please check your travel plans before lodging your visa application
                                                                        at the Portuguese Embassy. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please lodge your application at the Embassy where you will be staying the longest, OR the first point
                                                                        of entry if traveling with equal number of days. 
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person and by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online
                                                                        by the applicant.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Schengen visas have to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the main purpose of travel is. Therefore, please check your travel plans before lodging your visa application
                                                                        at the Portuguese Embassy. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please lodge your application at the Embassy where you will be staying the longest, OR the first point
                                                                        of entry if traveling with equal number of days. 
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person and by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online
                                                                        by the applicant.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Standard (Tourism, Business, Private Visit etc.)]
VFS service fee (inclusive of GST) is charged per visa application in addition to the applicable visa fee.
All fees can be paid by cash or Debit cards at the Visa Application Centre.
Cheque payments and direct bank transfers are not accepted.
All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
                                                                    
Instructions for applicants below 18 years:
- For all children below 18 years, both parents need to be present at the Visa Application Centre to sign the application form.
- All applicants below 18 years, travelling alone or with one parent must submit a signed letter of consent or authorization form and passport copy of the parent not travelling
Click here for Application Form. The form is in three languages, print the pages as per your selection
Click here to find the list of the required documents
Click here for TERMO DE RESPONSABILIDADE

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 2 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth
                                                                        in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The average processing time for Short term Schengen visa applications is 15 calendar days, a period which may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application in needed. but applicants are advised to leave at least 15 days between the appointment date and the travel date as some applicants/nationalities take longer for the Embassy to process.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Portugal in Singapore.

visa_types.name: Temporary stay (3 months to 1 year)
visa_types.documents_required:
Please find the checklist for the required documents below:
Temporary stay visas (stay of more than 3 months and less than 1 year)
E1 - Medical Treatment
E2 - Transfer of Workers to the World Trade Organization (WTO)
E3 - Independent Work Purpose
E4 - Scientific research, academic teaching, and highly qualified activity purposes (for less than 1 year)
E5 - Amateur Sports Purposes
E6 - Study, Study Exchange, Unpaid Professional Internship, Voluntary Service and International agreement with PT, or exceptional circumstances
E7 - Accompany a family member undergoing medical treatment
E8 - Seasonal Work Purpose
E9 - Academic or Professional Training Course 
E10 - Accompany a family member holding a Temporary Stay Visa
E11 - Exercise of a Professional Activity Done Remotely - Digital Nomads
visa_types.visa_fees:
Applicants have to pay visa fees at the Visa Application Center. Payment can be made by cash or NETS in Singapore Dollars (SGD)

[Table:   Temporary stay (3 months to 1 year)]
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for residence permit can take up to 45 calendar days.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Portuguese authorities.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A temporary stay visa is necessary for those wishing to stay in Portugal for more than 90 days and less than 1 year. The Embassy of Portugal in Singapore is the receiving office for temporary
                                                                        stay visa applications for Portugal.
The application should be submitted in the applicant’s country of usual residence or home country.
For information about temporary stay visas, please go to Visa information for Temporary Stay in Portugal (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/national-visas/necessary-documentation/temporary-stay)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A temporary stay visa is necessary for those wishing to stay in Portugal for more than 90 days and less than 1 year. The Embassy of Portugal in Singapore is the receiving office for temporary
                                                                        stay visa applications for Portugal.
The application should be submitted in the applicant’s country of usual residence or home country.
For information about temporary stay visas, please go to Visa information for Temporary Stay in Portugal (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/national-visas/necessary-documentation/temporary-stay)

## Visa Fees
Applicants have to pay visa fees at the Visa Application Center. Payment can be made by cash or NETS in Singapore Dollars (SGD)

[Table:   Temporary stay (3 months to 1 year)]

## Documents Required
Please find the checklist for the required documents below:
Temporary stay visas (stay of more than 3 months and less than 1 year)
E1 - Medical Treatment
E2 - Transfer of Workers to the World Trade Organization (WTO)
E3 - Independent Work Purpose
E4 - Scientific research, academic teaching, and highly qualified activity purposes (for less than 1 year)
E5 - Amateur Sports Purposes
E6 - Study, Study Exchange, Unpaid Professional Internship, Voluntary Service and International agreement with PT, or exceptional circumstances
E7 - Accompany a family member undergoing medical treatment
E8 - Seasonal Work Purpose
E9 - Academic or Professional Training Course 
E10 - Accompany a family member holding a Temporary Stay Visa
E11 - Exercise of a Professional Activity Done Remotely - Digital Nomads

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 2 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken less than 6 months ago
- Taken against a light (white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth
                                                                        in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The processing time for residence permit can take up to 45 calendar days.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Portuguese authorities.

## Application Form
Please download your application form
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Portuguese visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.

visa_types.name: Short stay Schengen visas (up to 90 days)
visa_types.overview:
Citizens of some non-EU countries are required to hold a valid Schengen visa when travelling to the Schengen area, which is a group of European countries that allows a person, who’s been admitted
                                                            to the Schengen Area, to travel without any further form of border control within the Schengen Area.
Current Schengen States are: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, the Netherlands,
                                                            Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
A Schengen visa is a short stay visa, i.e. for stays up to 90 days, allowing its holder to circulate in the Schengen Area.
The Embassy competent for examining and deciding on an application for a uniform visa shall be in the following order:
- The Schengen Member State of destination of the visit(s));
- The Schengen Member State whose territory constitutes the main destination of the visit(s) in terms of the length or purpose of stay; or
- If no main destination can be determined, the Member State of the first entry
The documentation related to the means of travel and accommodation must cover the complete itinerary of the single trip to the Schengen Area; i.e. printouts of intended flight bookings or other proof
                                                            of intended ways of travel, not only for the travel from and to the Schengen Area, but also for the travel between the different Schengen States, when more than 1 Schengen State will be visited
                                                            during the single trip.
Each Schengen visa application must fully comply with the documents required for the respective visa category and can only be submitted up to 6 months before the intended departure date.
The acceptance of a Schengen visa application and supporting documents by VFS does not guarantee the issuance of a Schengen visa.
The issuance of a Schengen visa, the Schengen visa validity period, the number of entries and amount of travel days are at the sole discretion of the Mission.
If a visa application is returned by the Mission requesting missing documents or any other missing/ incorrect information, the applicant must accept responsibility for any delay that may occur in
                                                            the visa application processing time.
                                                        
Holders of valid Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control upon arrival in the Schengen Area and are not necessarily guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries, even though
                                                            they may hold a valid Schengen visa for these countries.
visa_types.full_text:
Citizens of some non-EU countries are required to hold a valid Schengen visa when travelling to the Schengen area, which is a group of European countries that allows a person, who’s been admitted
                                                            to the Schengen Area, to travel without any further form of border control within the Schengen Area.
Current Schengen States are: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, the Netherlands,
                                                            Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
A Schengen visa is a short stay visa, i.e. for stays up to 90 days, allowing its holder to circulate in the Schengen Area.
The Embassy competent for examining and deciding on an application for a uniform visa shall be in the following order:
- The Schengen Member State of destination of the visit(s));
- The Schengen Member State whose territory constitutes the main destination of the visit(s) in terms of the length or purpose of stay; or
- If no main destination can be determined, the Member State of the first entry
The documentation related to the means of travel and accommodation must cover the complete itinerary of the single trip to the Schengen Area; i.e. printouts of intended flight bookings or other proof
                                                            of intended ways of travel, not only for the travel from and to the Schengen Area, but also for the travel between the different Schengen States, when more than 1 Schengen State will be visited
                                                            during the single trip.
Each Schengen visa application must fully comply with the documents required for the respective visa category and can only be submitted up to 6 months before the intended departure date.
The acceptance of a Schengen visa application and supporting documents by VFS does not guarantee the issuance of a Schengen visa.
The issuance of a Schengen visa, the Schengen visa validity period, the number of entries and amount of travel days are at the sole discretion of the Mission.
If a visa application is returned by the Mission requesting missing documents or any other missing/ incorrect information, the applicant must accept responsibility for any delay that may occur in
                                                            the visa application processing time.
                                                        
Holders of valid Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control upon arrival in the Schengen Area and are not necessarily guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries, even though
                                                            they may hold a valid Schengen visa for these countries.

## Overview
Schengen visas have to be obtained from the Embassy of the country where the main purpose of travel is. Therefore, please check your travel plans before lodging your visa application
                                                                        at the Portuguese Embassy. In case of travel to multiple Schengen countries, please lodge your application at the Embassy where you will be staying the longest, OR the first point
                                                                        of entry if traveling with equal number of days.
Every applicant, including children/infants, has to submit his/her Schengen visa application in person and by appointment only on their appointment date, which needs to be booked online
                                                                        by the applicant.

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Transit]
VFS service fee (inclusive of GST) is charged per visa application in addition to the applicable visa fee.
All fees can be paid by cash or Debit cards at the Visa Application Centre.
Cheque payments and direct bank transfers are not accepted.
All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Kindly refer to the list of requisite documents below before submitting your application; this will minimize chances of rejection of your Visa Application due to incorrect or inadequate
                                                                        documents.
                                                                    
Click here for Application Form. The form is in three languages, print the pages as per your selection
Click here to find the list of the required documents

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 2 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth
                                                                        in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The average processing time for Short term Schengen visa applications is 15 calendar days, a period which may be extended up to a maximum of 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application in needed. but applicants are advised to leave at least 15 days between the appointment date and the travel date as some applicants/nationalities take longer for the Embassy to process.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Portugal in Singapore.

visa_types.name: Residency (more than 1 year)
visa_types.documents_required:
Please find the checklist for the required documents below:
Residency visas (stay of more than 1 year)
D1 - Subordinate Work Activity
D2 - Independent Professional Activity or Migrants Entrepreneurs
D3 - Teaching Purposes, Highly Qualified Subordinated/Cultural Activity
D4 - Study, Higher Education, Students Exchange, Internships, Volunteer Work and Research
D6 - Family reunification (requires previous authorization from authorities in Portugal)
D7 - Retirement purposes, religious purposes or for people living out of individual revenue
D8 – RESIDENCY VISA FOR ACCOMPANYING FAMILY MEMBER APPLYING FOR A RESIDENCE VISA 
D9 - Exercise of a Professional Activity Done Remotely - Digital Nomads
visa_types.visa_fees:
Applicants have to pay visa fees at the Visa Application Center. Payment can be made by cash or NETS in Singapore Dollars (SGD)

[Table:   Residency (more than 1 year)]
visa_types.processing_time:
The processing time for residency visas can take up to 60 calendar days.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of thePortuguese authorities.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
A residency visa is necessary for those wishing to stay in Portugal for more than 1 year. Residency visas are valid for a period of 4 months, during which the visa holder must apply for
                                                                        a residency permit in Portugal.
The Embassy of Portugal in Singapore is the receiving office for residency visa applications for Portugal.
The application should be submitted in the applicant’s country of usual residence or home country.
For information about residency visas, please go to Visa information for Residencyin Portugal (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/national-visas/necessary-documentation/residency)
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A residency visa is necessary for those wishing to stay in Portugal for more than 1 year. Residency visas are valid for a period of 4 months, during which the visa holder must apply for
                                                                        a residency permit in Portugal.
The Embassy of Portugal in Singapore is the receiving office for residency visa applications for Portugal.
The application should be submitted in the applicant’s country of usual residence or home country.
For information about residency visas, please go to Visa information for Residencyin Portugal (https://vistos.mne.gov.pt/en/national-visas/necessary-documentation/residency)

## Visa Fees
Applicants have to pay visa fees at the Visa Application Center. Payment can be made by cash or NETS in Singapore Dollars (SGD)

[Table:   Residency (more than 1 year)]

## Documents Required
Please find the checklist for the required documents below:
Residency visas (stay of more than 1 year)
D1 - Subordinate Work Activity
D2 - Independent Professional Activity or Migrants Entrepreneurs
D3 - Teaching Purposes, Highly Qualified Subordinated/Cultural Activity
D4 - Study, Higher Education, Students Exchange, Internships, Volunteer Work and Research
D6 - Family reunification (requires previous authorization from authorities in Portugal)
D7 - Retirement purposes, religious purposes or for people living out of individual revenue
D8 – RESIDENCY VISA FOR ACCOMPANYING FAMILY MEMBER APPLYING FOR A RESIDENCE VISA 
D9 - Exercise of a Professional Activity Done Remotely - Digital Nomads

## Photo Specifications
Please provide 2 recent colour photographs, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken less than 6 months ago
- Taken against a light (white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and the face should cover at least 80% of the picture
- Size 3,5 x 4,5 cm
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth
                                                                        in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The processing time for residency visas can take up to 60 calendar days.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of thePortuguese authorities.

## Application Form
Please download your application form
Make sure you are on time for your appointment; for those showing up late there is no guarantee that the application can be submitted and applicants may need to reschedule their appointment.
                                                                        Kindly, notify the Portuguese visa application centre if you are unable to make your appointment.
